description: Operationalized Re_swarm as dimensionless ratio from session telemetry (git log + SESSION-LOG). Tested 5 non-tautological formulations against L+P outcome. Found regime separation but simpler model outperforms.
expect: Re_swarm computable from SESSION-LOG. Re_crit separates focused from chaotic sessions. Laminar >2x L+P. R²>0.3.

simple_commits_comparison.finding: Simple commit count outperforms all Reynolds formulations

overhead_alone.finding: Overhead fraction has zero predictive power for L+P

domex_commits.finding: DOMEX commit count barely predicts L+P

expert_momentum.finding: DOMEX × domains / concurrent has zero correlation

actual.key_finding: Reynolds analog works as regime classifier (2.6x L+P separation at Re_crit=0.6) but dimensionless ratio (R²=0.16) underperforms simple commit count (R²=0.37). Viscosity (concurrent sessions) and drag (overhead) are orthogonal to production quality — velocity alone predicts output. The ratio's power comes from its velocity component, not from the dimensionless normalization.

diff: Predicted R²>0.3 — got R²=0.160 for best Reynolds, but simple commits hits R²=0.370. Predicted laminar >2x — confirmed (2.59x). Did NOT predict that overhead and concurrency would be orthogonal to quality (r≈0 for overhead alone). Did NOT predict that simple commit count would outperform the dimensionless ratio. The Reynolds analog adds interpretive structure (regime classification) but not predictive power beyond its simplest component.
methodology: Non-tautological design: L+P excluded from all Re inputs to avoid circular correlation. 5 formulations tested. Threshold sweep for Re_crit optimization. Pearson correlation + regime separation ratio.
